# Luigi Corsaro, PhD

> Computational systems engineer for clinical genomics and epigenomics. Turns research code (WGS, NGS, DNA methylation, EWAS) into reproducible, validated and audit-ready computational systems. PhD in Genomic Data Science (University of Pavia, 2025) combined with 30 years of risk-critical systems engineering. Works remotely, worldwide.

## About

Luigi Corsaro works at the intersection of genomics and epigenomics, computational systems engineering, and hands-on delivery. His contribution is connective: propagating constraints across clinical, wet-lab, bioinformatics and software-engineering domains until they resolve into one system that runs, is understood, and can be handed over. He does not replace the domain specialists; he owns the interfaces between them.

Languages: Italian (native), English (professional), Chinese (basic).

## Services

- **From research code to production systems** — turning R and Python analysis into modular, documented, containerised workflows for WGS, NGS and EWAS.
- **Reliability, validation and audit-readiness** — reproducibility, traceability, quality control and provenance for genomics and methylation systems.
- **Fractional technical lead and handover** — architecture, validation strategy, mentoring and knowledge transfer, so the work outlives the engagement.

## Pages

- [Home](https://drake69.github.io/): positioning, services, engagement model.
- [Publications](https://drake69.github.io/papers.html): peer-reviewed papers, each linking to its DOI.
- [Projects](https://drake69.github.io/projects.html): open-source R packages and data products.

## Selected work

- First-author publications on epimutation scores in ER-positive breast cancer (Cancers, 2023) and stochastic epimutations in exposed populations (Environmental Epigenetics, 2024).
- SEMseeker: an R package for EWAS based on stochastic epimutation (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5095416).
- Seven peer-reviewed papers (two as first author) and a PhD thesis (University of Pavia, 2025).
